title: Maintain mould level 80-100mm above SEN tip for standard low-carbon rebar
Mould level below 60mm causes slag entrainment and longitudinal strand cracks
rationale: Steel surface gets too close to powder layer, gets pulled into meniscus

title: Mould level above 130mm causes splash on meniscus and false radar readings
Run tighter mould level window 70-85mm for peritectic grades (0.10-0.16% carbon)
Use 8mm peak-to-peak oscillation at 200 strokes/min for normal grades
Reduce to 6mm oscillation at 180 strokes/min for peritectic grades
Oscillation mark depth over 0.4mm indicates wrong amplitude or stroke rate
Run casting speed 4.2-4.8 m/min for 130x130 billets on 0.20-0.30% carbon rebar grades
Casting speed below 4.0 m/min causes transverse corner cracks from uneven shell thickening
Casting speed above 5.0 m/min pushes metallurgical length past bender entry causing internal cracks during straightening
Keep speed × section size below 650 for clean billets (metallurgical length proxy)
Reduce casting speed by 0.3-0.4 m/min for high-Mn grades (over 1.4% manganese)
High-Mn grades segregate hard at centerline requiring slower casting for columnar dendrites to reach center
Target water-to-steel ratio 0.9-1.1 litres per kg for standard grades in secondary cooling
rationale: Ensures proper cooling profile to avoid reheat cracking and thermal gradient issues

title: Water-to-steel ratio below 0.7 causes surface temperature overshoot above 1100°C leading to reheat cracking
rationale: Insufficient cooling allows surface temperature to exceed reheat cracking threshold

title: Water-to-steel ratio above 1.3 causes internal cracking from excessive thermal gradient
rationale: Excessive cooling creates thermal stress between surface and core

title: Maintain zone-2 flow rate 240-280 litres per minute for 130mm sections
rationale: Critical flow rate for proper cooling in first secondary cooling zone

title: Replace zone-2 nozzles when flow reduced by more than 30%
rationale: Prevents hot spots that cause longitudinal score lines visible on descaler entry camera

title: Check secondary cooling nozzles every shift change
rationale: Early detection prevents clogged nozzle hot spots leading to breakouts

title: Use basic flux (basicity 1.1, viscosity 1.5 poise at 1300°C) for low-carbon grades
scope: MiDa Rebar Mill - mould flux selection

title: Use acidic flux (basicity 0.9, viscosity 2.0-2.5 poise at 1300°C) for higher-carbon grades
scope: MiDa Rebar Mill - mould flux selection

title: Sticker breakouts caused by poor lubrication from wrong flux or cold meniscus preventing powder melt
Bleed breakouts past mould exit caused by insufficient shell thickness from high speed or low cooling
Mould thermocouple delta-T spike of 4-5°C indicates impending breakout with 30-60 seconds warning
scope: MiDa Rebar Mill - breakout early detection

title: Low-to-high carbon transitions create 4-6 meter transition zone that must be cut at runout
rationale: Mixed material zone occurs during carbon increase transition

title: High-to-low carbon transitions contaminate 8-12 meters with residual carbon from tundish washout
rationale: Residual carbon takes time to wash out of tundish

title: Tag transition pieces with paint and segregate to dedicated scrap bin
rationale: Prevents contaminated material from entering quality stream

title: Reduce speed by 0.5 m/min for first heat after peritectic-to-peritectic transitions
rationale: Mould hot spots from prior peritectic haven't normalized causing new strand cracking

title: Run 12 heats per tundish for standard grades, 8 heats for peritectic grades
Use acoustic test to check tundish life - change when sound goes from clear ring to dull thud
scope: MiDa rebar line tundish life assessment

title: Change tundish immediately if visual cracks appear on wear surface regardless of heat count
Perform borescope inspection at heat 6 on every tundish even if visual inspection is clean
scope: MiDa rebar line tundish inspection protocol

title: Limit SEN nozzle to maximum 6 heats on aluminium-killed steels, 8 heats on calcium-treated
scope: MiDa rebar line SEN nozzle life management

title: Run silicon-killed grades up to 10 heats on same SEN nozzle
scope: MiDa rebar line SEN nozzle life management

title: Aluminium-killed steels cause SEN nozzle wear through alumina deposition
scope: MiDa rebar line metallurgy effects on equipment

title: Target mould powder consumption 0.45-0.55 kg per square metre of strand surface for standard grades
Powder consumption below 0.35 kg/m² indicates stuck feeder or powder sintering on meniscus causing insufficient lubrication
Check powder consumption every 30 minutes comparing against calculated rate based on speed and section
Powder consumption variance over 15% from calculated rate is a warning flag
Maintain powder layer thickness 30-50mm on meniscus
Dip-test powder layer thickness with steel rod only during stable casting, never during transitions or speed changes
Run argon shrouding at 50-80 litres per minute in protective tube during ladle to tundish pour
processArea: Casting operations on the MiDa rebar line
scope: Argon shrouding for ladle to tundish transfer
rationale: Excess argon creates turbulence causing tundish slag entrainment

title: Maintain minimum 5 minutes residence time in tundish for inclusion flotation, 8 minutes for special bar quality steel
processArea: Casting operations on the MiDa rebar line
rationale: Shorter residence time prevents inclusions from floating out before reaching SEN

title: Use turbulent inhibitor pad in tundish to break up incoming stream for special bar quality steel
processArea: Casting operations on the MiDa rebar line

title: Without turbulent inhibitor pad, short-circuiting occurs and inclusions reach SEN before floating out
processArea: Casting operations on the MiDa rebar line

title: Turbulent inhibitor pad typical life is 4-6 heats before cracking
processArea: Casting operations on the MiDa rebar line

title: Cracked turbulent inhibitor pad causes sudden change in tundish temperature stratification
processArea: Casting operations on the MiDa rebar line
scope: Tundish turbulent inhibitor pad failure indicator

title: Longitudinal corner cracks caused by over-cooling in zone 2
rationale: Operator directly explained defect cause-and-effect relationship

title: Star cracks caused by copper pickup from mould tube past service life
rationale: Specific defect-to-cause relationship with equipment reference

title: Retire mould tube at 800-1200 heats based on heat count not visual condition
rationale: Clear maintenance rule with specific heat count criteria

title: Transverse face cracks are mould oscillation marks that propagated
Centre cracks caused by casting speed too high
Sub-surface inclusions caused by tundish shroud problems or insufficient residence time
rationale: Multi-cause defect explanation with process parameters
